summary: User shared a screenshot showing git push failure: 'origin' does not appear to be a git repository. Diagnosed root cause as missing origin remote in their execution environment (git remote -v returned empty, branch was 'work'). Local workspace on network share has origin configured and is on main branch. Provided fix: git remote add origin URL, then git push -u origin work, plus notes on auth and branch naming.
current_status: Portfolio repo exists locally with origin -> https://github.com/kkyliebi/kylie-bi-portfolio.git on main branch. User's push error is due to unconfigured remote in a separate session (likely Cursor agent/sandbox on work branch).
